title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
Top Reasons To Consider A Term Plan For A Young Family
At the beginning of your career, you may choose to divert a chunk of your income towards investing for your personal goals. However, this may change once you start a family. Eventually, your partner and your children may become your priorities. This might lead you to invest your income towards their safety and well-being. The first step you may want to take for this initiative is opting for a term plan for a young family, like yours. Let’s look at some reasons why you may consider buying a term plan at a young age.
Reasons to Get a Term Plan for a Young Family
Let’s look at the reasons listed below that may help you understand why buying a term plan may help your family.
Premiums are Affordable
Let’s consider an example. Divya, 27, and Ronald, 34, are co-workers. Divya has been married for a year and is considering starting a family, whereas Ronald has been married for 5 years and has 2 kids. While Divya’s husband also earns, Ronald is the sole earner for his family. Since Divya is considering starting a family, she understands the gravity of the added responsibility that might fall on her and her husband. On the other hand, Ronald knows that if something unfortunate were to happen, his family could face financial uncertainty.
Both Divya and Ronald decide to buy term plans for their respective family. However, both Ronald and Divya may have to pay different premiums for the same sum assured, considering factors like age, medical history, etc. When you buy life insurance at a younger age, the premiums could be low as compared to when you buy at an older age.
Creation of a Financial Safety Net
The rising cost of living due to the increase in inflation can impact everyone alike. If in your absence, your family faces the impact of such a rise in the cost of living, they may face financial uncertainty. As an earning individual, you might think your savings are sufficient to sustain your family in your absence. You may want to reconsider this decision if your savings might get used up for sudden emergencies. If you wish to build a financial corpus that might help your family remain financially stable for a good while, you may consider opting for a life insurance plan. The sum assured in the policy can create a safety net for your loved ones. If something unfortunate were to happen to you, the amount they may receive from the policy could help them in managing the cost of living while fulfilling life goals, such as child’s education and marriage, your partner’s financial independence or repayment of outstanding debts, etc.
Lower chance of rejection
As you grow old, your body may go through significant changes. For example, if you are planning to buy a term plan at the age of 55, you might have developed health conditions by then. You may be required to undergo a medical check-up by your insurer. Your health condition could impact your application status or your premiums.
At a younger age, you might still be in your prime when it comes to health and the insurer may not see you as a liability. Therefore, opting for the plan early on may prove beneficial.
Saving Tax1
When you buy a term plan, you may be able enjoy tax benefits on the premium paid and the benefits received as well, as per provisions of the Income Tax Act,1961. Premium of up to Rs. 1.5 lakhs per year, paid towards a term plan, is eligible for tax deduction under Section 80C of the Income Act, 1961. Additionally, if your nominee files a claim post your demise, the death benefit received is exempted under Section 10(10D) of the Income Tax Act, 1961, as per provisions stated therein. It is imperative for you to keep in mind that these benefits are subject to changes in the tax law.
Conclusion
The reasons given above may prove to be a helpful guide to you in clearing your confusion about whether you should consider buying a term plan for yourself, your partner, or your family at a young age or not. Before you buy the plan, you may want to take the assistance of the life insurance calculator. Using this calculator, you can get a quote for the plan based on your requirement.
Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)
What is the minimum age for buying life insurance?
The minimum age for buying a life insurance plan is usually 18 years depending upon the type of plan and the insurer.
Is a medical check-up required?
Under various scenarios, an insurance company may require you to undergo medical tests to assess your health conditions and fitness.
Should I opt for a long-term plan?
Before you decide on the term of the plan you wish to buy, make sure you are clear about your requirements. If you feel a long-term plan might benefit you and your loved ones, you may go for it. If you feel that a shorter-term duration is ideal, you may go for a short-term plan.
References:
1https://incometaxindia.gov.in/pages/acts/income-tax-act.aspx
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. full_pdf_text:
Top Reasons to Consider a Term Plan For Young People
At the beginning of your career, you may choose to divert a chunk of your income towards investing for your personal goals. However, this may change once you start a family. Eventually, your partner and your children may become your priorities. This might lead you to first invest your income towards their safety and well-being. While making such investment you might consider opting for a term insurance plan for a family, like yours.
Let’s look at some reasons why you may consider buying a term plan at a young age.
Reasons to Get a Term Plan for Young People
Let’s look at some of the reasons listed below that may help you understand why buying a term plan may help your family.
1. Premiums are Affordable when you are Younger
Let’s consider an example. Divya, 27, and Ronald, 34, are co-workers. Divya has been married for a year and is considering starting a family, whereas Ronald has been married for 5 years and has 2 kids. While Divya’s husband also earns, Ronald is the sole earner for his family. Since Divya is considering starting a family, she understands the gravity of the added responsibility that might fall on her and her husband. On the other hand, Ronald knows that if something unfortunate were to happen, his family could face financial uncertainty.
Both Divya and Ronald decide to buy term plans for their respective families. However, Ronald may have to pay a higher premium for the same sum assured as compared to Divya, considering the age difference. However, there are other factors too that may affect the premium like medical history, current illnesses, if any, etc. Hence said, when you buy life insurance at a younger age, the premiums could be low as compared to when you buy at an older age.
2. Creation of a Financial Safety Net
Your family may face financial uncertainty in case of your untimely demise. As an earning individual, you might think your savings will be sufficient to sustain your family in your absence. You may want to reconsider this decision, considering the sudden emergencies that might take place. If you wish to build a financial corpus that might help your family remain financially stable for a good while in your absence, you may consider opting for a life insurance plan. The sum assured in the policy can create a financial safety net for your loved ones. If something unfortunate were to happen to you, the amount they may receive from the policy could help them in managing the cost of living while fulfilling life goals, such as child’s education and marriage, your partner’s financial independence or repayment of outstanding debts, etc.
3. Lower chance of rejection
As you grow old, your body may go through significant changes. For example, if you are planning to buy a term plan at the age of 55, you might have developed health conditions by then. Your health condition could impact your life insurance application status or your premiums.
At a younger age, you might still be in your prime when it comes to health. Therefore, opting for the plan early on may prove beneficial.
4. Saving Tax1
When you buy a term plan earlier in your life, you may be able enjoy tax benefits on the premium paid and the benefits received as well, from an early stage, as per provisions of the Income Tax Act,1961. Premium of up to Rs. 1.5 lakhs per year, paid towards a term plan, is eligible for deduction under Section 80C of the Income Act, 1961. Additionally, if your nominee files a claim post your demise, the death benefit received is exempted under Section 10(10D) of the Income Tax Act, 1961, as per provisions stated therein. It is imperative for you to keep in mind that these benefits are subject to changes in the tax law.
Conclusion
Some of the reasons given above may prove to be a helpful guide to you in clearing your confusion about whether you should consider buying a term plan for yourself, your partner, or your family at a young age or not. Before you buy the plan, you may want to take the assistance of the life insurance calculator. Using this calculator, you can get a quote for the plan based on your requirement.
Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)
1. What is the minimum age for buying life insurance?
The minimum age for buying a life insurance plan is usually 18 years depending upon the type of plan and the insurer. 2. Is a medical check-up required?
Under various scenarios, an insurance company may require you to undergo medical tests to assess your health conditions and fitness. 3. Should I opt for a long-term plan?
Before you decide on the term of the plan you wish to buy, make sure you are clear about your requirements. If you feel a long-term plan might benefit you and your loved ones, you may go for it. If you feel that a shorter-term duration is suitable, you may go for a short-term plan. References:
1https://incometaxindia.gov.in/pages/acts/income-tax-act.aspx
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale.
